pathway_description: Meteorological or agricultural drought reducing water availability during the cropping season.
stress_description: Farmers report insufficient water for crop production, particularly for rabi and zaid seasons or during dry spells in kharif.

aer_references: AER-7: Deccan (Telangana) Plateau and Eastern Ghats, hot semi-arid ecoregion | states=['Andhra Pradesh', 'Telangana', 'Odisha (parts)'] | rainfall=740-960mm | aquifer=crystalline_basement

AER-8: Eastern Ghats, Tamil Nadu Plateau and Deccan (Karnataka), hot semi-arid to arid ecoregion | states=['Tamil Nadu (interior)', 'Karnataka (southern)', 'Andhra Pradesh (eastern)'] | rainfall=500-900mm | aquifer=crystalline_basement

condition.qualitative_description: Three or more severe drought weeks during the kharif season, or a combination of at least one severe week and four or more moderate drought weeks, indicating sustained crop water stress.

diagnostic_signals.explanation: Persistent severe drought weeks during kharif directly cause crop failure by depleting soil moisture below wilting point during critical growth stages. In semi-arid hard rock plateau zones (AER-7, AER-8) receiving 500–960 mm annually, even short intense dry spells can be catastrophic because shallow soils over crystalline basement have low water-holding capacity. SPI-based drought classification aligned with the India Drought Manual identifies severe drought (SPI < -1.5) as the threshold beyond which rainfed crop yields collapse. Studies on kharif rice show SPI is significantly correlated with productivity index, confirming that severe drought weeks are a reliable signal of crop water failure.

condition.qualitative_description: Four or more weeks in the kharif season where the 4-week moving window rainfall is less than 50% of historical average, indicating prolonged disruption to crop growth stages.

diagnostic_signals.explanation: Dry spells within an otherwise adequate monsoon season are a primary cause of agricultural drought in semi-arid India. In plateau and hilly terrain with shallow hard rock soils, the root zone moisture reserve is exhausted within 2–3 weeks of below-normal rainfall. Four or more dry spell weeks spanning critical crop stages (tillering, flowering, grain fill) cause irreversible yield loss. Management literature identifies dry spell duration as a key determinant of whether buffering via root system depth or mulching can compensate for rainfall deficit.

condition.qualitative_description: 3-month SPI below -1.0 (moderate to severe meteorological drought) co-occurring with VCI below 40 (moderate vegetation stress), confirming both meteorological and agricultural drought are active.

diagnostic_signals.explanation: The India Drought Manual recommends combining SPI (meteorological drought severity) with VCI (vegetation condition index derived from NDVI) to classify drought type and severity. SPI < -1.0 confirms below-normal precipitation, while VCI < 40 confirms that vegetation is already stressed, indicating the meteorological deficit has translated into agricultural drought. Studies across Allahabad, Kanpur and Lucknow districts found SPI and VCI positively correlated with kharif rice productivity index (r = 0.61–0.70), validating this combined signal for semi-arid Indian conditions.

condition.qualitative_description: Seasonal rainfall below 75% of historical mean, or annual rainfall below 80% of historical mean, confirming a below-normal precipitation year consistent with meteorological drought.

diagnostic_signals.explanation: In AER-7 and AER-8 zones (annual rainfall 500–960 mm), inter-annual variability is high and below-normal years are a primary trigger of water scarcity. Seasonal breakdown distinguishes kharif deficit (monsoon failure) from rabi deficit (post-monsoon irrigation dependency). A seasonal deficit below 75% of the mean is consistent with India Drought Manual thresholds for declaring meteorological drought. Annual precipitation trends also reveal whether drought is episodic or part of a multi-year drying pattern, which changes the urgency and type of intervention required.

condition.qualitative_description: Monsoon onset delayed by more than 14 days relative to the historical normal onset date for the district, shortening the effective kharif growing season.

diagnostic_signals.explanation: Delayed monsoon onset in semi-arid plateau zones compresses the kharif season, forcing farmers to either delay sowing (increasing exposure to terminal drought at grain fill) or sow into dry soil (risking germination failure). A delay of more than two weeks is considered agronomically significant for short-duration varieties of sorghum, groundnut and pulses common in AER-7 and AER-8. Delayed onset also reduces the total seasonal rainfall accumulation, amplifying the effect of any mid-season dry spells. Crop ecology literature identifies phenological synchronisation between crop water demand and soil moisture supply as a key determinant of yield under drought.

overall_reasoning_note: Drought-driven water scarcity in semi-arid hard rock plateau zones should be confirmed by at least two converging signals: (1) severe or moderate drought weeks (sig_01), (2) prolonged dry spells (sig_02), and (3) combined SPI + VCI confirmation (sig_03). Seasonal and annual precipitation deficits (sig_04) provide the background context, while delayed monsoon onset (sig_05) amplifies but does not independently confirm the pathway. If sig_03 shows low SPI but VCI is normal, consider whether the vegetation stress is masked by irrigation from tanks or borewells — in that case, groundwater stress may be the primary pathway. Always verify whether the reported water scarcity is concentrated in rabi/zaid (pointing to irrigation source failure) or kharif (pointing to monsoon failure), as this distinction changes the recommended intervention from water harvesting to drought-tolerant variety promotion.

confounders.confounder: Groundwater depletion causing water scarcity that mimics drought symptoms, particularly in rabi season when farmers rely on well irrigation
confounders.how_to_distinguish: Check seasonal_precipitation_mm for the kharif season — if monsoon rainfall was near-normal but rabi water scarcity is reported, the pathway is more likely groundwater stress than drought. Drought pathway is confirmed when kharif seasonal deficit (sig_04) co-occurs with dry spell weeks (sig_02) and SPI < -1.0 (sig_03). If SPI is near-normal but well levels are low, groundwater stress is the primary pathway.

confounders.confounder: Poor soil water retention due to degraded soils or lack of soil and water conservation structures, causing apparent drought even in near-normal rainfall years
confounders.how_to_distinguish: If annual_precipitation_mm and seasonal_precipitation_mm are within 10% of historical means but dry_spell_weeks is high and VCI is low, the cause may be rapid runoff from degraded soils rather than meteorological drought. In this case, drought_weeks_severe should be low or absent. Confirm by asking farmers whether nearby check dams or tanks filled during the monsoon — if yes, rainfall was adequate and soil/infrastructure degradation is the amplifying factor rather than drought itself.

recommended_solutions: Check dam repair using community labour ahead of monsoon
Emergency pre-monsoon water harvesting structure construction

sources.title: Drought assessment for kharif rice using standardized precipitation index (SPI) and vegetation condition index (VCI)
sources.relevance_note: Validates SPI and VCI as complementary indices for diagnosing meteorological and agricultural drought in kharif season; demonstrates significant correlation between SPI/VCI and kharif rice productivity index across semi-arid Indian districts, supporting sig_01 and sig_03 thresholds.

sources.title: Management of crop water under drought: a review
sources.relevance_note: Reviews crop water management under drought, identifying dry spell duration, phenological synchronisation with soil moisture supply, and site-specific stress hydrology as key determinants of yield loss; supports sig_02 (dry spell weeks) and sig_05 (monsoon onset delay) thresholds.

sources.title: Manual for Drought Management
authors: Government of India, Department of Agriculture, Cooperation and Farmers Welfare
sources.relevance_note: Defines India's official drought classification framework using SPI, MAI and VCI; specifies thresholds for meteorological and agricultural drought declaration used in sig_03 and sig_04; provides the basis for drought_causality_json variable interpretation.

sources.title: A review of drought concepts
sources.relevance_note: Reviews meteorological and agricultural drought concepts, including SPI thresholds (moderate: -1.0 to -1.49; severe: -1.5 to -1.99) and the propagation from meteorological to agricultural drought; supports threshold values used in sig_01 and sig_03.
